results.name: Salivary amylase
results.prediction_scope: Folded object: one salivary amylase protein chain; Ca2+/Cl- and substrate are not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is protein chain A only; cofactors, waters, and partner molecules are stripped unless a real result supplies them.

results.name: GFP
results.prediction_scope: Folded object: one GFP protein chain; chromophore chemistry is not modeled by the sequence-only fold.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is protein chain A only; waters and chromophore/ligand records are stripped so the preview matches the predicted object.

results.name: Myoglobin
results.prediction_scope: Folded object: one myoglobin protein chain; the heme group is not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is chain A protein atoms only, keeping the Mol* object aligned with the monomer prediction.

results.name: Carbonic anhydrase
results.prediction_scope: Folded object: one carbonic anhydrase II protein chain; catalytic Zn2+ is not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is chain A protein atoms only; the metal is an important biological context note, not part of the predicted coordinate output.

results.name: Phosphoglycerate kinase
results.prediction_scope: Folded object: one PGK protein chain, benchmarked with a deliberately shallow MSA.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is chain A protein atoms only; substrate and nucleotide state are not modeled.

results.name: Adenylate kinase
results.prediction_scope: Folded object: one adenylate kinase chain; ligand-bound open/closed states are biological context, not a folding movie.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 4AKE chain A only; chain B, waters, and ligands are stripped so the preview matches the single-chain prediction.

results.name: Lysozyme
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one 129-residue lysozyme chain; its 4 disulfide bonds are inferred geometry, not modeled covalent bonds.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1LYZ chain A protein atoms only. Bundled offline Fold needs a generated demo cache; until then, Fold requires the local backend.

results.name: Calmodulin
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one 148-residue calmodulin chain; the 4 bound Ca2+ ions are not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1CLL chain A protein atoms only (Ca2+ stripped). Bundled offline Fold needs a generated demo cache; until then, Fold requires the local backend.

results.name: Ras GTPase
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one 166-residue H-Ras G-domain; the bound GTP-analog (GppNHp) and Mg2+ are not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 5P21 chain A protein atoms only (nucleotide and metal stripped). Bundled offline Fold needs a generated demo cache; until then, Fold requires the local backend.

results.name: Ubiquitin
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one 76-residue ubiquitin chain; it is a single compact domain with no cofactors.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1UBQ chain A only. Bundled offline Fold needs a generated demo cache; until then, Fold requires the local backend.

results.name: Villin headpiece
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be the 36-residue HP36 subdomain; with no MSA it is a hard, low-signal case.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1VII chain A only. Run without evolutionary search (single sequence), so expect modest confidence - the point is the physics story, not a clean fold.

results.name: TIM barrel
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one 247-residue TIM monomer; the active enzyme is a dimer and the substrate is not modeled.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1TIM chain A protein atoms only; the biological dimer partner and substrate are omitted.

results.name: Alpha-synuclein
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. There is no single native fold: in solution alpha-synuclein is intrinsically disordered. The 1XQ8 preview is only its micelle-bound helical state.
results.omitted_context: Preview/prediction mismatch is the lesson: 1XQ8 is a membrane-mimic conformation, while the free protein is a disordered ensemble. Treat any single predicted structure here with suspicion.

results.name: Prion protein
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one PrP chain: a disordered N-terminal region plus a small helical C-terminal domain. The pathogenic PrP-Sc conversion is not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1QLX chain A only. The flexible N-terminus should read low pLDDT; the dangerous mechanism here (one sequence refolding into an infectious beta-rich state) is invisible to a single structure prediction.

results.name: p53 DNA-binding domain
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one p53 DNA-binding domain (~219 aa); the DNA it grips and the structural Zn2+ are not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is the 1TUP protein chain only; the bound DNA double helix and the zinc ion are stripped. A monomer fold cannot show the protein-DNA interface that is the whole point of p53.

results.name: HIV-1 protease
results.prediction_scope: Preview-first reference. Folded object would be one 99-residue protease monomer; the active enzyme is a homodimer and the bound inhibitor is not predicted.
results.omitted_context: Reference preview is 1HSG chain A only (the second chain and the indinavir inhibitor are stripped). The catalytic site forms between two chains, so a single-chain fold cannot show the working active site.
